verdict.summary: The change correctly replaces a detached, fixed-iteration poller with sampling tied to the lifetime of each in-flight HTTP response and bounded sequential retries. The biased select polls the response before yielding, while the yield branch gives server tasks opportunities to register the query; a removed registration remains unobservable across all attempts, so the mutation-sensitive assertion is not weakened. Requests are sequential and share the same AppState-backed registry, making the expected peak of one valid. No correctness, soundness, security, or vacuity issue is evident in the diff.
